$schema_note: TIM-2342: Curated industry benchmarks the BP narrative LLM is allowed to cite as src="benchmark". Keep this list short, sourced, and load-bearing — every entry will be passed verbatim into the prompt. When the data grows (TIM-1698), this file becomes a seed; the loader will then read from a real dataset. Until then, the narrative is forbidden from inventing a benchmark not in this file.

benchmarks.label: Specialty coffee blended COGS
benchmarks.source: Specialty Coffee Association annual cafe operating reports (2023, 2024)
benchmarks.note: Blended across espresso, brewed, and food. Pure-espresso cafes trend toward 25%; food-forward cafes toward 33%.

benchmarks.label: Specialty coffee labor as % of revenue
benchmarks.source: Specialty Coffee Association cafe benchmarking, Toast Restaurant Industry Report 2024
benchmarks.note: Steady-state. Ramp-period labor runs higher because fixed staffing absorbs into thinner revenue.

benchmarks.label: Specialty coffee rent (occupancy) as % of revenue
benchmarks.note: 8% is the commonly-cited lender expectation. Tertiary-market cafes can sit at 4-6%; flagship urban locations at 10-12%.

benchmarks.label: US specialty coffee average ticket
benchmarks.source: Toast Restaurant Industry Report 2024, Square Café data 2024
benchmarks.note: Drink-only tickets cluster near $6-$7; food-attached tickets near $9-$11. Drive-through-heavy cafes trend lower.

benchmarks.label: Neighborhood-cafe daily customer count (steady state)
benchmarks.source: Specialty Coffee Association cafe benchmarking; trend reports from National Coffee Association
benchmarks.note: Lower end for new-to-market neighborhood cafes in their first year; upper end for established or higher-traffic locations.

benchmarks.label: Morning-daypart share of cafe revenue (6am-11am)
benchmarks.note: Lender-recognized concentration. Plans should explicitly address how staffing and equipment throughput handle the morning peak.

benchmarks.label: Cafe build-out cost (2nd-generation vs raw shell)
benchmarks.note: Lower end for second-generation cafe space; upper end for full conversion of raw shell incl. mechanical, plumbing, hood.

benchmarks.label: Recommended opening cash buffer
benchmarks.source: SBA small-business lending standards; SCA guidance
benchmarks.note: Lenders look for at least 3 months of operating expense covered by an opening cash buffer line in the use-of-funds.

benchmarks.label: Commercial/SBA debt-service coverage ratio threshold
benchmarks.note: 1.20x is the SBA floor; commercial banks often look for 1.25x. Below 1.10x is generally unbankable without significant equity.

benchmarks.label: Cafe revenue ramp to steady state
benchmarks.note: First-month revenue typically lands at 40-60% of steady-state; reaches 90%+ by month 9-12.

benchmarks.label: Cafe food-program waste
benchmarks.note: Lower end with active end-of-day markdown and pastry-only food programs; upper end with full prepared-food programs and weak demand forecasting.

benchmarks.label: Cafe inventory turns (specialty coffee + food)
benchmarks.note: Includes green and roasted coffee, milks, pastries-in-house. Shops with house-roasted coffee skew higher.
